steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The skill is subject-verb agreement. The subject of the blank is "each one of these plays" — the word "each" makes this singular regardless of the prepositional phrase that follows, so the verb must be singular.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘focuses’: singular present-tense verb — agrees with the singular subject "each one." ✓
  • Choice B — ‘focus’: plural present-tense verb — violates subject-verb agreement; "each one" is singular, not plural. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘are focused’: plural present-tense passive verb — violates subject-verb agreement for the same reason as B. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘were focused’: plural past-tense passive verb — violates subject-verb agreement (plural) and introduces past tense that is inconsistent with the present-tense context of the sentence. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
